title: Peripherally inserted central catheter systems, devices, and methods thereof for pediatrics
abstract: A catheterization system for treatment of patients can include a peripherally inserted central catheter comprising a catheter tube joined to an extension leg with a junction and a securement device that fits around a portion of the junction to inhibit movement. The catheter tube can include a taper proximate the junction, the taper transitioning the catheter tube from a larger outer diameter and a larger wall thickness to a smaller outer diameter and a smaller wall thickness. The taper and thicker wall thickness near the junction can help prevent damage to the catheter tube near the junction when bent (e.g., to secure a proximal region of the catheter to a patient's arm). The securement device can configured to fit around an outer diameter of the junction to inhibit movement of the junction when the junction is held in the securement device.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A peripherally inserted central catheter for a neonatal patient, comprising: a catheter tube including a tapered portion, the tapered portion: extending from a proximal end of the catheter tube to a medial portion of the catheter tube, and including a first outer diameter with a first wall thickness at the proximal end of the catheter tube and a second, smaller outer diameter with a second, thinner wall thickness at the medial portion of the catheter tube; one or more extension legs; and a junction coupling the proximal end of the catheter tube to the one or more extension legs, the junction including a circumferentially recessed portion about an outer diameter of the junction configured to interlock with a securement device to inhibit movement of the junction when the peripherally inserted central catheter is inserted in a neonatal patient. 2. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1, wherein the first wall thickness is sufficiently thick to prevent breakage about the proximal end of the catheter tube when the peripherally inserted central catheter is handled or inserted in a neonatal patient in accordance with instructions for use of the peripherally inserted central catheter. 3. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1 , wherein the tapered portion of the catheter tube is up to about 10% of a total length of the catheter tube. 4. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 3, wherein the tapered portion of the catheter tube is more than about 5% of a total length of the catheter tube. 5. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1, wherein the catheter tube includes markings every centimeter along a length of the tapered portion of the catheter tube and markings every 2 mm or 2.5 mm along a 3-cm length of the catheter tube proximate the tapered portion to assist a medical practitioner in pull-back adjustments when positioning the catheter. 6. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1, wherein the catheter tube has a French size up to about 2 Fr distal to the tapered portion of the catheter tube. 7. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1, wherein the catheter tube includes a thermoplastic polyurethane. 8. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 1, the junction includes one or more tapered lumens configured to match lumen diameters of the one or more extension legs and the catheter tube in the junction. 9. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 8, wherein the catheter tube is a single-lumen catheter tube. 10. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 9, wherein the lumen of the single-lumen catheter tube is configured for a flow rate up to about 5.0 mL/min. 11. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 8, wherein the catheter tube is a dual-lumen catheter tube. 12. The peripherally inserted central catheter of claim 11, wherein each lumen of the dual-lumen catheter tube is configured for a flow rate up to about 1.5 mL/min. 13. A catheterization system for treatment of neonatal patients, comprising: a peripherally inserted central catheter, comprising: a catheter tube joined to one or more extension legs with a junction, the catheter tube including a taper proximate the junction, the taper transitioning the catheter tube from a first outer diameter and a first wall thickness to a second outer diameter smaller than the first outer diameter and a second wall thickness smaller than the first wall thickness in a proximal to distal direction; and a securement device configured to fit around a recessed portion around an outer diameter of the junction to inhibit movement of the junction when the junction is held in the securement device. 14. The catheterization system of claim 13, wherein the taper is configured to prevent the catheter tube from breaking at or proximate the junction. 15. The catheterization system of claim 13, wherein the first wall thickness is at least three times as thick as the second wall thickness. 16. The catheterization system of claim 13, wherein the securement device includes a suture wing forming a U-shaped portion configured to sit in the recessed portion of the junction when the junction is held in the securement device. 17. The catheterization system of claim 16, wherein the suture wing removably attaches to the junction. 18. The catheterization system of claim 13, wherein the peripherally inserted central catheter is a dual-lumen catheter. 19. A method, comprising: obtaining a catheterization system, the catheterization system comprising: a catheter comprising a catheter tube joined to one or more extension legs with a junction, the catheter tube including a taper proximate the junction, the taper transitioning the catheter tube from a first outer diameter and a first wall thickness to a second outer diameter smaller than the first outer diameter and a second wall thickness smaller than the first wall thickness in a proximal to distal direction; and a securement device configured to fit around a recessed portion of an outer diameter of the junction to inhibit movement of the junction when the junction is held in the securement device; accessing an opening in a patient's skin; navigating the catheter to a desired location in the patient's body; attaching the securement device to the junction; attaching the securement device to the patient's skin; and infusing a fluid into the patient. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising extracting a liquid from the patient.
